text: Setting clear project goals is essential for success, and using the SMART framework can help. SMART stands for Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of saying "improve the website," a SMART goal would be "increase mobile page load speed by 20% within three months." It is also important to break the project into milestones, which are key checkpoints along the way, such as completing a design prototype. Each milestone leads to a deliverable, like a report or a finished product feature. Finally, stakeholder alignment means ensuring everyone involved agrees on the goals and priorities. This avoids confusion and keeps the team focused on the same outcomes.
